title: Viewer bridges path-level memory to code files
summary: Path level memory such as affects path: src can leave combined viewer graphs looking disconnected from actual code files because path nodes do not canonicalize to file:src/... code nodes. The viewer should add capped vir
body:
Path-level memory such as affects_path: src can leave combined viewer graphs looking disconnected from actual code files because path nodes do not canonicalize to file:src/... code nodes. The viewer should add capped virtual affects_code_path edges from memory packets to representative matching code files at merge/render time, preserving compact persisted graph artifacts while making memory-code relationships visible. Audit should report path-level and precise memory-code links separately so broad grounding is not misreported as zero. Verified by npm --prefix mcp test, git diff --check, and a 20k-file benchmark viewer hook check showing 24 affects_code_path edges and 17 visible memory-code edges under the 90-node cap.
Verified by: npm --prefix mcp test; git diff --check; 20k benchmark viewer hook check
